summary: Justin Brennan conducts live broker outreach calls across AR/TN/AL markets, demonstrating deal sourcing methodology and how to build relationships with brokers who control off-market inventory. He emphasizes using Crexy as a broker Rolodex tool, identifying market makers by logo frequency, and positioning oneself as a serious buyer through operational setup (CRM, lenders, mock deals).
takeaways: Identify market makers by scanning Crexy logos repeatedly—same brokers appearing multiple times indicate they control deal flow

Target 10-30 unit B/C class assets, ideally built before 2000s, priced $50k-$150k per unit to control capital (e.g., 20 units @ $100k/unit = $2M deal, $600k down payment, $60k out-of-pocket after acquisition fee)
Use the initial property call as a 'lure'—don't just pitch that deal; mention broader market interest ('we're looking at coming into Arkansas') to trigger broker to share off-market pipeline
Keep buy box high-level conversationally; don't send formal buy box immediately, build rapport first over time
Underwrite on fundamentals: 65-70% LTV, mid-teens IRR target, 8-15% economic loss depending on market (exceptions only in CA, S. Florida, Northeast at 5-6%)

Set up full operational infrastructure (website, CRM, lenders with soft quotes, mock deals, advisory board) so brokers see you as serious; this unlocks off-market deal access after in-person market trips
Follow up aggressively: send aggressive LOIs to create conversation hooks, then follow up with brokers to discuss numbers rather than waiting passively

skill_candidates.change: Implement systematic logo-scanning on Crexy to identify 2-3 market makers per target market before calling
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin demonstrates scrolling through Crexy thumbnails, noting 'Moses and Tucker,' 'PWA,' 'Matthews'—'the ones you see the most logos of repeatedly, those are your market makers'

skill_candidates.change: Establish price-per-unit floor of $50k-$150k and validate against fundamental underwriting (65-70% LTV, mid-teens IRR, 8-15% economic loss) before engaging broker
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin rejects the $147k/door 10-unit as 'probably not going to work,' explains capital efficiency math: '$100k per unit on 20 units = $2M, $600k down, $60k OOP after acquisition fee,' and tells Mark the 8-unit at $100k/door fails because 'it doesn't work at 80-85% financing'

skill_candidates.change: Schedule in-person market trips to meet brokers face-to-face after phone rapport, moving from virtual to 'boots on the ground' to unlock off-market inventory
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin: 'when you're doing your market setup...meet them in face to face because that's when it goes from a phone call and a Zoom...to a real human that now okay I feel comfortable sending you deals'

title: Weekly Coaching_04/23/26 | Justin Brennan
